assertions.title: CLI run subcommand executes a benchmark from config

steps.expected: Config loaded, 2 tasks executed, results manifest produced, exit code 0
steps.observed: Config loaded, 2 tasks executed (smoke-echo-pass: SUCCESS score=1.0, smoke-echo-fail: FAILURE score=0.0), manifest with schema_version V1 printed to stdout, exit code 0

assertions.title: CLI provides structured output format option

steps.action: Run `uv run grist-mill run --config configs/examples/smoke.yaml --output-format json`
steps.observed: JSON manifest with name, version, timestamp, schema_version, total_tasks, and tasks array. Parsed successfully with json.loads.

steps.action: Run `uv run grist-mill run --config configs/examples/smoke.yaml --output-format yaml`
steps.observed: YAML manifest with all fields intact. Parsed successfully with yaml.safe_load.

assertions.title: End-to-end from task generation through evaluation to export

steps.expected: All tests pass — TaskResult round-trips through export without data loss
steps.observed: 40 tests passed in 0.12s. Tests cover JSON/CSV export, telemetry preservation (tokens, latency, tool calls, cost, raw events), error category preservation across all categories, cross-format consistency (record counts, task IDs, scores), and pipeline traceability (task ID and status preserved through full pipeline).

steps.expected: Registry resolves for all subsystems; deleted artifact produces clear errors
steps.observed: 9 registry cross-subsystem tests passed: registry resolves for harness config, binder, agent context; deleted artifact errors in all consumers; all subsystems see same state; deregister mid-session invalidates all; registry isolation no side effects.

steps.action: Run `uv run pytest tests/test_cross_tool_binding.py -v --tb=short` (environment isolation tests)
steps.expected: Same tool invocation succeeds in both local and Docker modes
steps.observed: 18 environment isolation and tool trace tests passed: same task/artifacts in local mode, Docker config artifact context, agent context independent of runner type, harness consistent artifact flow with local runner, MCP cleanup in local and exception modes, tool deregistration, consistent tool traces across runs, artifact context identical for different runner types, binder context independent of harness runner type, end-to-end registry to harness, deregistered artifact prevents harness run, artifact type filtering across subsystems.

frictions.description: CLI `--output-format json/yaml` emits progress messages (e.g., 'Running 2 task(s)...', 'Completed: 1 passed...') to stdout alongside the structured output, which means piping stdout directly to a JSON/YAML parser fails. The structured output is mixed with progress text.
frictions.resolution: Extracted the structured portion from the output by finding the JSON start delimiter ('{') or filtering progress lines. The structured manifest itself is valid JSON/YAML.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. VAL-CLI-02 confirmed CLI run executes benchmarks with exit code 0. VAL-CLI-06 confirmed JSON and YAML output formats produce valid structured manifests (minor friction: progress messages mixed into stdout). VAL-CROSS-01 confirmed 40/40 tests pass for end-to-end eval-to-export round-trip. VAL-CROSS-09 confirmed 9/9 registry cross-subsystem tests pass with clear error on artifact deletion. VAL-CROSS-12 confirmed 18/18 environment isolation tests pass with consistent tool binding across local/Docker modes.
